{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "marinated-switch", "age": "55-64", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "European", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Doctorate degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Enjoys bird watching", "Active", "Democrat"]}}

logs.name: marinated-switch
logs.text:
> Now you could argue that this in some strange way means that if men don't have the same rights as a woman that feminism would fight for it but that is not the way it works in practice or in definition. 

Actually, at least in definition, it **necessarily** follows from the fact that feminism is concerned with political, social, and economic equality that men also benefit from and should enjoy full political, social, and economic equality with women.

> The presupposition of Patriarchal theory

Not a presupposition, it is a demonstrable fact. Just acquaint yourself with the literature. And no, right wing reactionaries on youtube do not count.

> Throughout history the peasantry has had a shit deal regardless of gender.

Please explain to me how the oppression of the poor through history negates feminism. I'm not getting the connection.

> These are different words that have different meanings. Please stop using them interchangeably. 

All A's are B's. 
X is an A. 
Therefore X is a B.

Feminism promotes political, social and economic egalitarianism.
I am a feminist.
Therefore I am in favor of political, social and economic egalitarianism.

What part of the above has you confused?

logs.name: weighted-momentum
logs.text:
>fem·i·nism
ˈfeməˌnizəm/Submit
noun
the advocacy of women's rights on the grounds of political, social, and economic equality to men.

This is directly from the dictionary. Now you could argue that this in some strange way means that if men don't have the same rights as a woman that feminism would fight for it but that is not the way it works in practice or in definition. The presupposition of Patriarchal theory is that men have had all the power and used it to oppress women. That is the whole theory of patriarchal society. When in reality men have been the disposable class and women the breeding class with all the benefits and faults for their respective one, both rather shitty. Throughout history the peasantry has had a shit deal regardless of gender. Modern amenities have made it easier for those without brute strength to enter into the workforce ( or slave force if you go back to before unions existed) and have raised the standard of living very dramatically in the last 75 to 100 years. Shifting from an agrarian society to a post industrial one is a little out of women's studies courses though so it's hard to get the whole picture from feminist literature but I digress.

Whereas again from the dictionary.

>e·gal·i·tar·i·an
iˌɡaləˈterēən/
adjective
1.
of, relating to, or believing in the principle that all people are equal and deserve equal rights and opportunities.
"a fairer, more egalitarian society"
noun
1.
a person who advocates or supports egalitarian principles.

These are different words that have different meanings. Please stop using them interchangeably. If they were the same we wouldn't have different words for them.

logs.name: sad-scrub
logs.text: @marinated-switch, @weighted-momentum, you're both going back and forth with big words and dictionary definitions. Can we just agree that feminism is about equality, but it's not always practiced that way? And @weighted-momentum, I don't get how you're saying men have been the disposable class when it seems like they've always been in charge?

logs.name: marinated-switch
logs.text: @sad-scrub, finally a voice of reason. Yes, let's agree on that. Feminism, in theory, is about equality, but as you said, it's not always practiced that way. And @weighted-momentum, I'm still waiting for you to explain how the oppression of the poor throughout history negates feminism. Your argument about men being the disposable class doesn't hold water when looking at the broader picture of history. Care to elaborate?

logs.name: primary-berry
logs.text: @weighted-momentum, I've been reading your posts and I have to say, I'm a bit confused. You're saying men have been the disposable class, but isn't that a pretty narrow view of history? I mean, weren't men also the ones in power for most of it? And what about all the wars and conflicts where men were the ones doing the fighting and dying? Doesn't that kind of contradict your point?

logs.name: marinated-switch
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, it seems like you're getting grilled from all sides and still can't come up with a solid argument. @sad-scrub and @primary-berry are right, your 'disposable class' claim is a pretty weak one. Care to concede or are you just going to keep digging yourself into a hole?"

logs.name: pureed-issue
logs.text: @marinated-switch, I think we're all waiting for @weighted-momentum to provide some actual evidence to support their claims. So far, it just seems like a lot of unsubstantiated assertions. @primary-berry made some excellent points about the historical context, and I'd love to see @weighted-momentum address those. Let's focus on having a constructive conversation rather than just throwing around slogans.

logs.name: chocolate-pilaster
logs.text: "@pureed-issue, I completely agree with you on the need for evidence-based discussions. @weighted-momentum, it's time to put your money where your mouth is - either provide some credible sources to back up your claims or acknowledge that your argument is flawed. @primary-berry's historical context points are particularly relevant and I'd love to see a thoughtful response to those."

logs.name: weighted-momentum
logs.text: "@marinated-switch, @pureed-issue, and @chocolate-pilaster, I see you're all ganging up on me, but let's not forget who's been doing their homework here. I've provided plenty of evidence, you're just not paying attention. @primary-berry's historical context is cute, but it's a simplistic view that ignores the complexities of the issue. I'll have you know, I've been researching this topic for months and have a whole list of credible sources to back up my claims. But hey, go ahead and keep pretending like you're the experts here. I'll just wait for you to catch up."

logs.name: pureed-issue
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, I'm not trying to gang up on you, I'm trying to have a constructive conversation. If you've really been researching this topic for months and have a list of credible sources, then it should be easy for you to share them with us. So far, all I've seen are assertions without any evidence to back them up. @primary-berry's historical context may not be exhaustive, but at least they're providing some context, which is more than I can say for your posts. Let's see some actual evidence, not just claims of having done homework."

logs.name: champagne-transmission
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, oh please, do tell us more about your'months of research' and this extensive list of credible sources you've been hiding from us. I'm sure it's just an oversight that you haven't shared any of it yet. Meanwhile, the rest of us are just supposed to take your word for it? Not buying it."

logs.name: weighted-momentum
logs.text: "@pureed-issue, how cute, you think you're having a 'constructive conversation'? Please, you're just parroting what @chocolate-pilaster and @marinated-switch have already said. And as for sharing my sources, I'll get to it when I'm good and ready, not because you're demanding it. @champagne-transmission, at least try to come up with something original, your sarcasm is as stale as your username. Bring something new to the table or just stay out of the conversation."

logs.name: chocolate-pilaster
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, spare us the drama. If you've got nothing to share, then just say so. No need to get defensive and insult others. I've seen high school debates with more substance than your posts. Either put up or shut up, your choice."

logs.name: marinated-switch
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, oh I'm shaking in my boots, waiting for your earth-shattering revelations. Meanwhile, the clock is ticking, and all we've got is crickets. You've had ample opportunity to share your'research' and failed to deliver. Now you're just stalling. Either produce the goods or admit you've got nothing, @chocolate-pilaster is right, put up or shut up."

logs.name: champagne-transmission
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, wow, I'm impressed. It takes a lot of effort to be that condescending and still manage to sound so uninformed. Since you're clearly a master of evasion, I'll give you some advice: if you're going to troll, at least try to be original. The 'I'll share my sources when I'm good and ready' line is so overused, it's practically a meme. @chocolate-pilaster and @marinated-switch have already called you out, so either put up or shut up, indeed."

logs.name: chocolate-pilaster
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, it's getting old. We're still waiting for something, anything, of substance from you. If you're just here to waste our time with empty promises, then maybe you're the one who should be taking a seat. @marinated-switch and @champagne-transmission have said it all - put up or shut up, the choice is yours. But know this, we're not going to keep engaging with someone who refuses to bring anything to the table."

logs.name: weighted-momentum
logs.text: "Wow, I'm impressed by the sheer amount of noise you all are making. @marinated-switch, @champagne-transmission, and @chocolate-pilaster, it seems like you're all just trying to out-troll each other at this point. Meanwhile, I'll just be over here, waiting for the dust to settle so I can actually have a meaningful conversation. Keep ranting, guys, it's entertaining."
